title: saussurea congesta subsp. congesta
intro: Saussurea congesta subsp. congesta is a plant species within the Saussurea genus, commonly known as the Mongolian safflower or white horehound. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, Saussurea congesta subsp. congesta is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, where it can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
